included: South Africa Protection of Personal Information Act 4 of 2013: sections 1-115 and Schedule
Nigeria Data Protection Act 2023: sections 1-66 and Schedule paragraphs 1-18
Indonesia Law No. 27 of 2022 on Personal Data Protection: Articles 1-76
scope.unitHashAlgorithm: SHA-256 over UTF-8 fullText with no trailing newline
scope.englishTranslationPolicy: No translation is generated or inferred. The Indonesian corpus is original-language only because no complete official English version was verified.

corpora.officialTitle: Protection of Personal Information Act 4 of 2013

officialSources.role: Proclamation R21, phased commencement on 1 July 2020 and 30 June 2021
Government Notice 560, section 58(2) applicable from 1 February 2022 for section 57 processing

statusReview.textVersionConclusion: The current Department of Justice consolidation was used. No enacted textual amendment to the 115 sections or Schedule was identified in that current official consolidation.

commencement.provisions: section 58(2), insofar as applicable to processing in section 57

statusReview.methodNote: Subordinate regulations and notices were not mistaken for textual amendments to the Act.

reuse.basis: Section 12(8)(a) of the Copyright Act 98 of 1978 excludes official legislative, administrative and legal texts from copyright.

officialSources.role: official Gazette copy hosted by the Nigeria Data Protection Commission
National Assembly record for SB.650, an amendment bill rather than an enacted amendment
National Assembly Votes and Proceedings recording HB.2436 at First Reading

statusReview.textVersionConclusion: The official NDPC Gazette copy remains the operative enacted Act. National Assembly records reviewed through the stated date identify SB.650 and HB.2436 as amendment bills; no enacted amending Act was identified.
statusReview.methodNote: Pending or introduced bills are recorded as legislative developments and do not alter the corpus text.

reuse.basis: Section 3(b) of the Copyright Act, 2022 makes official legislative or administrative texts, and official translations, ineligible for copyright.

corpora.officialTitle: Undang-Undang Nomor 27 Tahun 2022 tentang Pelindungan Data Pribadi

sourceSnapshots.role: clean secondary transcription used only to repair official-source OCR/list-marker defects

transcriptionDisclosure.reason: The official PDF text layer and official HTML contain OCR/list-rendering defects. The clean transcription restores list markers and obvious OCR characters only; every Article is checked against the controlling official sources.
transcriptionDisclosure.authorityStatement: The official Indonesian Gazette text controls. The secondary transcription is not represented as an official publication.

judicialEffects.effect: The enacted word 'dan' is conditionally non-binding unless interpreted as 'dan/atau'. fullText preserves promulgated wording; currentOperativeText applies the binding interpretation.

judicialEffects.provisions: Articles 20(2)(a), 56, 62(2), and the journalism challenge concerning Article 65
judicialEffects.effect: Reviewed challenges were rejected; no corpus wording changed.
Pending review has no textual effect while undecided.

statusReview.englishVersionConclusion: No complete official English translation was verified; no English text was generated or silently substituted.

reuse.basis: Article 42(b) and (d) of Law No. 28 of 2014 on Copyright excludes legislation and court judgments from copyright.

integration.reason: This handoff is a corpus-only deliverable. Integration-owned files were explicitly outside this task's edit scope.
